error.signature: HAL_SPI_Transmit timeout: SPI TXE flag not set within timeout period

verdict.summary: SPI transfer times out waiting for TXE/RXNE flags. Clock not running, wrong mode, or NSS misconfiguration.

dead_ends.why_fails: If SPI clock is not running, no timeout value will help. The peripheral itself is misconfigured.

dead_ends.action: Switch from hardware NSS to software NSS without changing code
dead_ends.why_fails: Hardware vs software NSS requires different GPIO and CR register configuration; just toggling the mode breaks CS.

workarounds.action: Verify SPI clock is enabled in RCC
workarounds.how: __HAL_RCC_SPI1_CLK_ENABLE();  // must be called before any SPI init

workarounds.action: Check SPI mode (CPOL/CPHA) matches the slave device
workarounds.how: Check slave datasheet for clock polarity and phase. Mode 0 (CPOL=0,CPHA=0) is most common.

workarounds.action: Verify GPIO alternate function mapping for SCK, MOSI, MISO pins
workarounds.how: GPIO_InitStruct.Alternate = GPIO_AF5_SPI1;  // AF mapping varies by STM32 family and pin
